title: Card & MetricCard
intro: Two containers that look alike and do opposite jobs. `wf-card` is the default content chunk — a record, a panel, a list — with header/body/footer regions you compose freely. `wf-metric-card` is the dashboard specialist: one big number, one label, one optional trend. The whole entry is one decision — when each is the right reach (DESIGN.md §4.1).

blocks.body:
`wf-card` is the default content container. Reach for it whenever you have a discrete chunk of related content — a record, a summary panel, a settings group, a list — that needs visual separation from its siblings. It ships with a `--wf-card-bg` surface, a `--wf-card-border`, an 8px corner radius, and three optional regions: `wf-card-header`, `wf-card-body`, `wf-card-footer`. Compose only the regions you need; a body-only card is valid.

`wf-metric-card` is *only* for dashboard metrics — a single headline `wf-metric-value`, a `wf-metric-title`, and an optional `wf-metric-subtitle` carrying a trend delta. It is not a generic "highlighted card." The container-choice rule is sharp: if your card holds more than one metric, or any prose, it is a `wf-card`, not a `wf-metric-card` (DESIGN.md §4.1, §7.4).

Quick lookup from the decision tree: highlighted single metric → `MetricCard`. Discrete content chunk → `Card`. Form fields → `FormSection` (not covered here). Width control only → `Container`.

blocks.title: Card — Header / Body / Footer
blocks.caption: wf-card composed of header, body, and footer regions for richer container content. The header is a space-between flex row (title + action), the body holds free content, and the footer right-aligns its buttons on a tinted --wf-color-neutral-50 ground.

blocks.title: Card regions
blocks.intro: The structural slots inside a wf-card. Each is optional — use only the regions the content needs. Header and footer each carry their own divider border; the footer gets a subtle tinted ground.

rows.name: wf-card-header
rows.when: Title row at the top. Lays out as a space-between flex row, so a title block sits left and an action (Edit, Add) sits right. Carries a bottom border.
rows.code:
Contract Details

CNT-2024-001

rows.name: wf-card-body
rows.when: The main content region — 16px padding. Holds prose, fields, or a nested list. Set padding:0 inline when embedding a full-bleed wf-list.
rows.code: Supply agreement between Acme Corp and Global Industries for Q1 2024.

rows.name: wf-card-footer
rows.when: Action row at the bottom. Right-aligned flex with an 8px gap, a top border, and a --wf-color-neutral-50 ground. Park commit/cancel buttons here.

blocks.title: Card — Outlined vs Elevated
blocks.caption: Side-by-side outlined (border) and elevated (shadow) wf-card surface variants. The elevated variant drops its border to transparent and lifts its shadow on hover; the flat variant strips both.

blocks.title: Card surface & state variants
blocks.intro: Modifiers added alongside the base wf-card. Surface variants (default, elevated, flat) are mutually exclusive; clickable and selected stack for card-based selection UIs.

rows.name: wf-card (default)
rows.when: Outlined — a 1px --wf-card-border on a white surface. The workhorse; works on white or light backgrounds.

rows.name: wf-card-elevated
rows.when: Trades the border for a shadow (border-color goes transparent). Lifts to a deeper shadow on hover. Use for depth/hierarchy when the card floats above a tinted ground.

rows.name: wf-card-flat
rows.when: No border, no shadow. For nested cards or where layout already implies the grouping. Note: nesting cards is itself an anti-pattern (see Rules).

rows.name: wf-card-compact
rows.when: Tightens padding across all three regions (12px header/body, 8px 12px footer). For sidebars, dense lists, and data-heavy surfaces — the density default.

rows.name: wf-card-clickable
rows.when: Makes the whole card a target: cursor, 150ms transition, border turns --wf-color-primary on hover, scales to 0.99 on active. For selectable tiles and link cards.

rows.name: wf-card-selected
rows.when: The chosen state in a card-based selection. Primary border plus a #eff6ff tinted fill. Pairs with wf-card-clickable.

blocks.title: MetricCard — Trend Indicator
blocks.caption: wf-metric-card showing a single headline value with up/down trend delta indicator. The trend pill lives inside wf-metric-subtitle; ▲ green for up, ▼ red for down, — gray for neutral.

blocks.title: MetricCard structure & trend
blocks.intro: A metric card is header → value → subtitle. The subtitle is where the trend pill lives, alongside an optional comparison phrase like "vs last month." Trend direction maps to a dedicated semantic color token.

rows.name: wf-metric-header
rows.when: The label row — an optional wf-metric-icon plus the wf-metric-title (13px, secondary text). Sits above the value.

rows.name: wf-metric-value
rows.when: The headline number — 28px, 600 weight, primary text. The whole reason the card exists. Exactly one per card.

rows.name: wf-metric-trend-up
rows.when: Positive delta. Renders --wf-metric-trend-up (#16a34a) green. Pair the color with the ▲ glyph — never color alone (DESIGN.md §3.3).
rows.code: ▲ 12.5% vs last month

rows.name: wf-metric-trend-down
rows.when: Negative delta. Renders --wf-metric-trend-down (#dc2626) red, paired with the ▼ glyph.

rows.name: wf-metric-trend-neutral
rows.when: No meaningful change. Renders --wf-metric-trend-neutral (#6b7280) gray, paired with the — em-dash glyph.

blocks.title: MetricCard — Status Variants
blocks.caption: wf-metric-card status colorways (primary/success/warning/negative) tying the metric to a color intent via a 4px left accent border.

blocks.title: MetricCard status & size variants
blocks.intro: Status variants add a 4px colored left border to tie the metric to a semantic intent. Honor §3.1: the accent color is a status signal, not decoration — use it only when the metric actually reports that status.

rows.name: wf-metric-card-primary
rows.when: Marks the headline / current-focus metric. 4px --wf-color-primary (#2563eb) left border.

rows.name: wf-metric-card-success
rows.when: A healthy / passing metric. 4px --wf-color-success (#16a34a) left border. Not a decorative "good" accent.

rows.name: wf-metric-card-warning
rows.when: Attention-required / pending metric. 4px --wf-color-warning (#d97706) left border.

rows.name: wf-metric-card-error
rows.when: A failing / hard-fail metric. 4px --wf-color-error (#dc2626) left border.

rows.name: wf-metric-card-elevated
rows.when: Shadow instead of border (border-color transparent). For metric grids that float above a tinted ground.

rows.name: wf-metric-card-compact
rows.when: Tighter padding (16px) and a smaller 24px value. For secondary metrics or dense KPI strips.

blocks.title: Card & metric tokens
blocks.intro: The values both containers reach for. Surface and border tokens are shared; trend colors belong to the metric card.

rows.use: Surface fill for both wf-card and wf-metric-card.
1px outline on the default (outlined) variant of both cards.
Resting shadow for the elevated variant.
Deeper shadow on hover of an elevated wf-card.
Green text for a positive trend pill (wf-metric-trend-up).
Red text for a negative trend pill (wf-metric-trend-down).
Gray text for a no-change trend pill (wf-metric-trend-neutral).

blocks.title: Card with header, body, and footer
blocks.code:
Contract Details

CNT-2024-001

Supply agreement between Acme Corp and Global Industries for Q1 2024.

Active
blocks.note: Every region is optional — drop the header or footer for a body-only card. To embed a full-bleed wf-list, set the body to padding:0.

blocks.title: MetricCard with trend
blocks.code:
[icon] Total Revenue
$124,500
▲ 12.5% vs last month
blocks.note: One value, one title, one optional trend. The moment you reach for a second metric or a paragraph of prose, switch to wf-card.

blocks.title: Container choice (DESIGN.md §4.1)
blocks.intro: The whole point of this entry: which container, and when. Get this right and the rest follows.

items.why: A record, summary panel, settings group, or list that needs separation from its siblings is exactly what wf-card is for.
It is purpose-built for one big number, one label, one optional trend — and nothing else.

items.rule: More than one metric, or any prose, lives in a wf-card — never a wf-metric-card.
items.why: The metric card has no room for it; cramming prose in breaks its scan-at-a-glance job (§7.4).

items.rule: Don't reach for wf-metric-card as a generic "highlighted card."
items.why: Highlight is a job for wf-card with emphasis (a heading, a badge), not for the metric primitive.

items.rule: Status accent borders are status signals, not decoration.
items.why: wf-metric-card-success/-warning/-error follow §3.1 — apply the colorway only when the metric reports that actual status.

items.do: Two metrics → two separate wf-metric-cards in a grid
items.dont: Two values stuffed into one wf-metric-card
items.why: The metric card is one-number-only by contract (§7.4). Side-by-side cards stay scannable; a doubled-up card doesn't.

items.do: Prose + status + value → wf-card with body content
items.why: Any prose disqualifies the metric card — that content belongs in wf-card-body (§4.1).

items.do: Group a list inside one wf-card
items.dont: A wf-card nested inside another wf-card
items.why: Nested cards are two layers fighting for one job — pick the outer one (§7.1).
Color is never the only signal (§3.3) — the arrow glyph carries the direction for color-blind and AT users.

items.title: Elevated variants drop their border
items.detail: Both wf-card-elevated and wf-metric-card-elevated set border-color: transparent and lean entirely on the shadow. On a white page background the shadow can read faintly — elevated cards are designed to sit on a tinted ground (--wf-color-neutral-50 / -100), as the demos do.

items.title: wf-card-footer carries its own ground and divider
items.detail: The footer is a right-aligned flex row with a top border and a --wf-color-neutral-50 background baked in. Don't add your own divider or fill — you'll double it.

items.title: Full-bleed body content needs padding:0
items.detail: wf-card-body has 16px padding. To embed a wf-list edge-to-edge, set the body to padding:0 and strip the list's own border/radius — otherwise you get a border inside a border with a 16px gutter.

items.title: Trend glyphs are HTML entities, not icons
items.detail: The up/down/neutral indicators are literal characters — ▲ (▲), ▼ (▼), — (—) — inside the wf-metric-trend span. There's no icon component; the color class colors the whole pill including the glyph.

items.title: Status accent is a left border only
items.detail: wf-metric-card-success/-warning/-error/-primary add a 4px left border and nothing else — no fill, no text recolor. The metric value and title stay neutral, so the accent reads as a category stripe, not a full-card alarm.
